_note: Curated presentation data: the 7 clusters transcribed from the engine repo's FIELD.md (its own ranking and status labels, 2026-07 state), with the shipped works mapped onto them by the site's editor. The mapping mirrors FIELD.md's own filing where it names instruments (clusters 4 and 7) and the collective's dossier threads (material stakes = cluster 1); it is presentation, not the archive.

axis.left: Spectacle / data-sublime — AI and data as MEDIUM
axis.right: Investigation / evidence — AI and data as SUBJECT
axis.note: The one opposition that structures the field. Prestige and curatorial budgets moved to the investigation side 2024→2026; this project works that side.

clusters.name: Material & planetary AI cost
clusters.blurb: What computing physically costs the planet: energy, water, e-waste — and what the official accounting of that cost makes visible or conceals.

clusters.name: AI in war / algorithmic targeting
clusters.blurb: Detection and targeting systems whose misclassification kills. The collective has scoped a work here ("The Sample") and holds it — the verifiability ceiling is stated honestly on its workboard.

clusters.name: Counter-forensics / OSINT
clusters.blurb: Investigative aesthetics as evidence — satellites, open sources, courts. Unbuilt territory for this collective so far.

clusters.name: Provenance & authenticity
clusters.blurb: How do we know what is real — and do the tools that promise to tell us actually work? The collective's core series lives here: detectors, provenance standards and fraud tests put on trial.

clusters.name: Decolonial & more-than-human
clusters.blurb: AI as one cognition among many. Unbuilt territory for this collective so far.

clusters.name: Data justice & missing data
clusters.blurb: Who gets counted, who gets classified — and what a change in the diagnostic criteria does to the people it defines.

clusters.name: AI self-consumption
clusters.blurb: Turning AI's own logic against itself: benchmarks eaten by the models they measure, fairness criteria that are mathematically impossible to satisfy at once.
